campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and overall money management. The council also discussed plans to improve transportation options around Campbell, looking at different ways residents can get around the city.
keyTopics: City's investment performance and financial transactions for July
Overall treasurer's report and cash management
Multimodal transportation plan (buses, biking, walking, and other travel options)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The Saratoga City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026 where residents could comment on a new rule or policy. After hearing from the public, the council introduced a new ordinance (a city rule) and decided to skip the usual first-reading delay, so they can vote to officially adopt it at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input
New ordinance introduced and first reading waived
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next council meeting
Expedited approval process used for this measure

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026 to approve the official written records from several meetings held in March. The council also authorized the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc., though the specific project details are not fully shown in the agenda.
keyTopics: Approved minutes from six March meetings (special sessions, study sessions, and closed-door meetings)
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc. (project details not specified in available agenda)
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 14, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to handle several administrative matters and approve work on the city's water and wastewater systems. The council excused some members from the meeting, made appointments to city boards, and approved changes to engineering contracts and construction budgets for water recycling and wastewater facility projects.
keyTopics: Engineering contract amendments for the regional wastewater facility upgrades
Budget increase for the South Bay Water Recycling Pump Station 5 project
New appointments to city boards and commissions
Construction management agreements with HDR Engineering and CDM Smith

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 14, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting where they reviewed the city's spending plans for the next year, including which projects will get funded for improvements around town. They also officially recognized Teen Month and National Volunteer Week to highlight the contributions of young people and volunteers in the community.
keyTopics: Next year's budget for city construction and improvement projects
Teen Month recognition and celebration
National Volunteer Week proclamation
Planning session for fiscal year 2026-27 spending

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 14, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission met on April 14, 2026 to review meeting notes from March and discuss changes to tenant protection rules in Sunnyvale. The council introduced a new ordinance that would update the city's residential tenant protections program to give renters more safeguards.
keyTopics: Approval of previous meeting minutes from March 25, 2026
New ordinance amending tenant protection laws (Chapter 19.71)
Updates to residential tenant protections program
Housing and human services policy review

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 13, 2026
cupertino.summary: The City Council held a community workshop on April 13, 2026 to help residents learn about OpenGov, a new online tool that shows how the city spends its money. The council also discussed rules for lobbyists—people who try to influence city decisions—requiring them to register and report their activities.
keyTopics: OpenGov financial transparency tool launch and community training
New lobbyist registration and reporting requirements
How residents can track city spending online
Rules for people trying to influence city decisions

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 14, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 14, 2026, using a hybrid format that allows residents to attend in person or join remotely via Zoom. The city is making it easy for the public to participate and submit comments on agenda items before the meeting.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and remote Zoom participation available)
Remote participation via Zoom video conference
Phone dial-in option for those without internet access
Written public comments accepted before the meeting through eComments system

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to discuss updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and reviewed new project requests from individual council members. The council also considered changes to how council vacancies are filled and approved meeting minutes and calendars.
keyTopics: Updates to federal funding plans for housing and community development (2025-2030)
New project requests submitted by individual council members
Changes to the process for filling council vacancies
Approval of previous meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — April 9, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council held a public hearing about a building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown. The owner wants to upgrade the building's historic status, which would allow them to add 5,000 extra square feet of floor space as a reward for preserving and making the building safer against earthquakes.
keyTopics: 405 Kipling Street historic building upgrade request
5,000 square feet of bonus floor area for seismic improvements
Historic Resources Board meeting schedule and assignments
Downtown Commercial zone development approval
